title: Villages Denomination
description: Villages with names that are automatically generated.
body: ## Introduction: Villages Denomination is a mod that generates random names for villages when they are loaded into the world. These names can either be randomly generated or selected from a configured list. When you enter a village, its name will be displayed to the player, showing both the main name and a complement. For example: "Kakariko Village", where "Kakariko" is the name and "Village" is the complement. This complement can be changed in the settings or even removed, displaying only the village name. ## Integration: - Traveler Titles: Display the village name in the title. - Waystones: When you activate a waystone in a village, it will be renamed to the village name. ## Examples: ### Village Name ![villageName](https://gitlab.com/ragnardragus/villagesdenomination/-/raw/main/img/example02.gif) ### Traveler's Titles ![villageTitle](https://gitlab.com/ragnardragus/villagesdenomination/-/raw/main/img/example01.gif) ### Waystones ![waystone](https://gitlab.com/ragnardragus/villagesdenomination/-/raw/main/img/example03.png) ## Commands The mod provides some commands that can be executed if you have operator permissions: ```/villages all``` Displays all village names. ```/villages rename ``` Renames a village. ```/villages teleport ``` Teleports to the village. ## Configs: **Client-side:** - You can change the village name color changing the `villageNameColor` value. **Server-side:** - You can change the village name complement changing the `villageNameComplement` value. - You can set the list of village names changing the `customVillageNames` value. - You can set the blacklist of structures that be ignored when generating village names changing the `structuresBlacklist` value. ## Compatibility: It must be compatible with any other mod that adds villages to the game and register the village structure with "_village_" in id. Warning: This mod performs multiple file read and write operations on the server during runtime and has not been extensively tested. If you experience performance issues as a result, it is recommended that you stop using the mod and report the issue by creating an issue in the project's repository. ## Sharing: You are free to use this mod in your modpack or modify it for your own purposes. However, you may not redistribute or upload it to another site as if it were your own. If you're interested in using this mod in your project, you may choose to credit me or not, but if you do, I would greatly appreciate it.
